method: Dual-axis Supervised Contrastive Learning (SupCon, Khosla et al. 2020)
description: Two independent SupCon losses (reward bins + opponent hand) sum to train a single 15->64->64->8 encoder, with VICReg variance penalty to prevent collapse.
key_findings: SupCon + VICReg prevents 1D collapse: effective_dim=2 (vs 1 in hand-only triplet)
Simultaneously encodes both axes: hand probe 63.3% (matches hand-only), reward bin probe 55.4%
Reward Spearman rho (0.118) below contrastive_repr_v1 (0.163) — expected, categorical SupCon does not optimize continuous distance ordering
Two dominant PCA components (PC1=53%, PC2=30.5%) indicate two meaningful encoding axes
Per-axis SupCon formulation avoids batch sparsity of AND/OR joint logic in dual_axis_repr_v1
